creator: jostein@solidsystem.no
text:
(In reply to Steve Wills from comment #8)

I just tried running the pkg upgrade like you did with "sudo pkg upgrade" instead of inside "sudo -i", it worked fine then.

So I'm quite certain you will be able to reproduce if you run "sudo -i", and then "pkg upgrade".

Here is the env output inside "sudo -i":

PATH=/sbin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/root/bin
TERM=xterm-256color
SHELL=/bin/csh
LOGNAME=root
USER=root
USERNAME=root
MAIL=/var/mail/root
HOME=/root
SHELL=/bin/csh
SUDO_COMMAND=/bin/csh
SUDO_USER=jostein_stuhaug
SUDO_UID=1001
SUDO_GID=1001
HOSTTYPE=FreeBSD
VENDOR=amd
OSTYPE=FreeBSD
MACHTYPE=x86_64
SHLVL=1
PWD=/root
GROUP=wheel
HOST=freebsd-11-gcloud-issue.c.<redacted-projectid>.internal
REMOTEHOST=74.125.74.163
EDITOR=vi
PAGER=more
BLOCKSIZE=K
-------
